content: We can reframe the A2A multi-agent collaboration protocol as a distributed "working memory expansion system" that bypasses the individual agent's working memory capacity limit for reasoning. When any single agent hits its working memory bottleneck when processing complex multi-step collaborative tasks, the A2A protocol lets it offload intermediate reasoning chunks to other specialized agents, effectively creating a pooled, scalable working memory network for the whole multi-agent system.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
